name: The trails of the Forest of La Vallesa
name_original: Las sendas del Bosque de La Vallesa
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bosque de la Vallesa offer diverse terrain within El Turia Natural Park, characterized by a fragrant pine forest and Mediterranean riparian woodland. The area features undulating sections suitable for cross-country training, alongside downhill segments and banked turns for more experienced riders. Routes often include mountainous parts with elevation gains, and some pass by a picturesque reservoir. This region provides varied landscapes for mountain biking near Paterna, Valencia. Best mountain bike trails around Bosque de la Vallesa The most popular mountain bike trail is The trails of the Forest of La Vallesa, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route navigates the diverse terrain of the pine forest. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Vallesa Viewpoint – Las Colinas Trail loop from la Vallesa, an easy 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This trail offers views from La Vallesa Viewpoint and traverses rolling hills. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Sant Esperit Monastery – Path of the Eagle Peak loop from la Vallesa, a 41.7 miles (67.1 km) trail leading through extensive forest and past the Sant Esperit Monastery, often completed in about 4 hours 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Bosque de la Vallesa is defined by pine forests, diverse terrain with undulating and downhill sections, and historical features like an ancient aqueduct. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Bosque de la Vallesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bosque de la Vallesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: There are over 220 mountain bike trails in Bosque de la Vallesa, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore the diverse terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: Yes, Bosque de la Vallesa offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners and families. An excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vallesa Viewpoint – Las Colinas Trail loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 16.5 km path that offers scenic views and rolling hills. Another family-friendly choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34433334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Paterna from La Mola – Railway crossing at La Vallesa loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.9 km long and also rated easy.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: The trails in Bosque de la Vallesa feature diverse terrain, from undulating sections ideal for cross-country training to more challenging downhill segments and banked turns for experienced riders. You'll navigate through fragrant pine forests, Mediterranean riparian woodlands, and some routes include mountainous parts with elevation gains.

Q: Are there any points of interest or historical sites to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. Some routes pass by an ancient aqueduct, trenches, and bunkers from a past war. You might also glimpse the private estate of the Counts of Trenor, including the interior lake of La Vallesa de Mandor. The nearby Lloma de Betxí offers a fascinating Bronze Age settlement dating back to 1800-1300 BC.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: Bosque de la Vallesa is enjoyable for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Even in winter, the Mediterranean climate can provide good conditions. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Bosque de la Vallesa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vallesa Viewpoint – Las Colinas Trail loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32044026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sant Esperit Monastery – Path of the Eagle Peak loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: The trails in Bosque de la Vallesa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 500 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful pine forests, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels. The presence of historical features and natural landmarks also adds to the experience.

Q: Is there public transport access to Bosque de la Vallesa for mountain bikers?
A: Yes, Bosque de la Vallesa is accessible by public transport. You can reach the area by metro to Riba-roja de Túria, making it a convenient option for those without a car.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Bosque de la Vallesa offers routes with significant elevation gains and technical sections. A notable difficult trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32044026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sant Esperit Monastery – Path of the Eagle Peak loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 67.1 km and includes substantial climbing, often taking nearly 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike trails?
A: While the Bosque de la Vallesa itself is a natural park, there are towns and villages nearby, such as Paterna and Riba-roja de Túria, where you can find cafes, bars, and restaurants to refuel after your ride.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Bosque de la Vallesa?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural parks like Bosque de la Vallesa, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations or signage at trailheads to ensure compliance and respect for wildlife.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by a lake or reservoir?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes within Bosque de la Vallesa pass by a picturesque reservoir or lake, adding to the scenic beauty of the ride. The region research mentions a serene reservoir within the forest.
